latex:
The table below shows the amount of blue and yellow paint needed to make green paint.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
\multicolumn{3}{|c|}{Amount of Paint} \\
\hline
blue & yellow & green \\
\hline
6 & 2 & 8 \\
\hline
12 & 4 & 16 \\
\hline
18 & 6 & 24 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

The amount of blue paint is
answer_latex: three times the amount of yellow paint.
keep_mc_reason: The question asks for a relationship between blue and yellow paint amounts. Removing choices leaves a clean problem asking students to describe the multiplicative relationship.
diagram_notes: A table with 3 columns (blue, yellow, green) and 3 data rows. Header row shows 'Amount of Paint' spanning all columns. Data: Row 1: 6, 2, 8. Row 2: 12, 4, 16. Row 3: 18, 6, 24.
